category_title: High-achieving students who dominate group interactions and exclude lower-performing peers
minimal_context: The student is working on a multiple-choice test in a mixed-ability group during the second part of an introductory education course.
dialogue:
TUTOR: For the second part of our lesson, please work with your mixed-ability group to complete this multiple-choice test.

STUDENT: I already went faster and finished the first page. The answers are A, C, B, D, A.

TUTOR: Are you explaining the information to the rest of your group?

STUDENT: There is no need to listen to others. I can just go ahead with using my own answers.

TUTOR: Let's hear what the other group members have to say about the next question.

STUDENT: (Interrupts a peer) No, you're stuck. I am making the final decisions in choosing the right answers for the group because I am tired of being held back by slower teammates.

options.A: Pre-plan specific tutor responses to potential non-compliance during the second part of the 90-minute lesson.
options.B: Ensure the reinforcer is only offered after the desired behavior occurs, and not at other times during the lesson.
options.C: Combine the final grade for each student using 30% by peer assessment and 70% from the joint project.
options.D: Transition the decision of when and how long to take quiet time from the tutor to the student.
